rationale: The skill is a browser automation CLI that drives Chrome/Chromium via CDP, so it will spawn the agent-browser binary (or via npx) which in turn launches a Chrome/Chromium subprocess. It needs network access to arbitrary user-specified websites plus integrations like Slack, Vercel Sandbox, and AWS Bedrock AgentCore, so hosts are effectively wildcard. It reads/writes session state, auth vault, and screenshot/video artifacts under its config/cache dirs and the working directory, and the discovery stub itself instructs running 'agent-browser skills get core' to fetch workflow content.
